research.product: Splunk HTTP Event Collector (Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform)
research.interface: HEC JSON event protocol on /services/collector/event (current Splunk Enterprise 9.x/10.x docs)

official_sources.title: Format events for HTTP Event Collector | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: envelope keys event/time/host/source/sourcetype/index/fields; epoch <sec>.<ms> time format; batching multiple envelopes into one request body (concatenated JSON objects, newline separation legal); fields must be flat and only the /event endpoint indexes them

official_sources.title: HTTP Event Collector REST API endpoints | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the JSON event protocol endpoint /services/collector/event and its versioned /event/1.0 twin

official_sources.title: Troubleshoot HTTP Event Collector | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the HEC response code table used for the retry policy: 0/200 Success, 6/400 Invalid data format, 8/500 Internal server error, 9/503 Server is busy, 26/429 HEC queue is at capacity; official guidance that clients must act on response codes

research.language_rationale: The service being instrumented is a .NET 10 app. Splunk's .NET logging library (Splunk.Logging.*) is archived and predates modern .NET, so the credible integration surface is the documented HEC REST protocol over HttpClient — the same wire contract Splunk's maintained connectors implement.

verified_facts: HEC auth is the Authorization: Splunk <token> header; tokens never belong in URLs.
An event envelope carries event plus optional time/host/source/sourcetype/index/fields; time is epoch seconds with millisecond precision.
Multiple envelopes are batched into one request body as concatenated compact JSON objects; newline separation is legal and is what Splunk's own connectors emit.
fields must be a flat object of index-time custom fields and is only indexed when sent to the /event endpoint.
Success is 200 {"text":"Success","code":0}; 503 code 9 (Server is busy), 500 code 8 and 429 code 26 (queue at capacity) are the transient statuses; 400 code 6 (Invalid data format) is terminal.
Splunk documents no server-side batch size negotiation for HEC; senders bound their own request bodies (the sink's MaxBatchBytes) and preserve emit order.
